danger_signals.visual_form: Decreasing colored bar above character sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Player or opponent approaching defeat state

danger_signals.visual_form: Character animation transition to idle/taunting pose after health reaches zero
danger_signals.meaning: Round loss; transition to Finish Him/Her state

danger_signals.visual_form: Text overlay appearing when opponent is defeated but alive
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger state—opponent vulnerable to finishing move (Fatality); window for execution present

danger_signals.visual_form: Large monstrous character sprite with distinct silhouette
danger_signals.meaning: Sub-boss danger tier; significantly elevated combat threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Throne Room stage; distinct visual environment different from tournament stages
danger_signals.meaning: Final confrontation; highest narrative and mechanical danger

safety_signals.visual_form: Opponent health bar emptied; character plays victory animation
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary safety; player advances to next round or match

safety_signals.visual_form: Stage transition, opponent swap in endurance rounds
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression state; player survived tournament bracket advancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Transition to bonus challenge screen with material block and meter
safety_signals.meaning: Non-combat safe state; opportunity for points without direct threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tournament arena locations on Shang Tsung's Island
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Seven island stage environments serving as tournament venues; implies controlled/neutral tournament grounds

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Shang Tsung as tournament organizer/final boss; Goro as enforcer/sub-boss
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Goro as monstrous threat distinct from human fighters
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Throne Room stage (final arena); implicit control over tournament infrastructure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Seven selectable fighter characters (Johnny Cage, Sonya Blade, Liu Kang, Kano, Jax, Sub-Zero, Scorpion)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mirror Match against player-character duplicate indicates faction self-opposition
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Multiple stage locations; suggests distributed fighter origins across Earthrealm

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arena stages with spectator environments; organized fight brackets
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Structured 1v1 matches with defined progression; endurance match sequences with health meter persistence across multiple opponents

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Goro positioned as undefeated champion; Shang Tsung as final adversary; Throne Room as ultimate stage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must defeat all seven characters, then three endurance matches, then sub-boss Goro, then Shang Tsung—hierarchical threat escalation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Tournament framing as existential stakes; 500-year narrative context of Goro's dominance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Single-player mode narrative structure implies defending realm through tournament victory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Absence of blood effects; modified or alternative Fatality animations compared to arcade version
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Johnny Cage alternate censored super-uppercut Fatality exists in code but disabled; reflects platform-specific world state (Nintendo content policy)

environmental_storytelling.example: Seven distinct island locations serve as tournament arenas; implies Shang Tsung's island is tournament headquarters and controlled territory

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss character progression as narrative escalation
environmental_storytelling.example: Goro's 500-year undefeated reign communicated through positioning as sub-boss; Shang Tsung as final opponent communicates he is true antagonist and realm threat

environmental_storytelling.technique: Character selection roster as faction identity
environmental_storytelling.example: Seven playable characters represent diverse fighter pool; Mirror Match implies self-doubt or inner conflict theme
Breaking progressively harder materials (wood→stone→steel→ruby→diamond) represents player skill escalation and tournament preparation

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Victory animation; health bar remains above zero; opponent health depletes
Health bar depletes to zero; character takes damage animations; gameplay control restricted
Character freezes or staggers in vulnerable idle state; accepts incoming Fatality finishing move without defense
Large sprite scale; monstrous appearance; positioned as mandatory encounter after endurance matches—communicates enhanced threat tier
Final tournament match opponent; positioned after Goro defeat; Throne Room arena stage suggests ultimate authority and power
Health bars, timer, round counter, stage/opponent information displayed persistently—communicates match state and tournament structure

notes: Mortal Kombat SNES (1993) operates primarily as a mechanical fighting game with minimal explicit world-state narrative integration during gameplay. Danger and faction communication relies heavily on implicit tournament structure and boss positioning rather than explicit visual storytelling. Key manifestation layer insight: the game communicates political/power hierarchy through difficulty curve (7 fighters → 3 endurance → Goro → Shang Tsung) and stage design. Censorship state (absence of blood/gore on SNES vs. arcade) represents a distinct world-state manifestation driven by external policy constraints. Character-level faction identity is weak in sources; most communication occurs at tournament/stage level. Mirror Match and endurance sequences are primary behavioral tells for world-state shifts.
